title: Check how much 14.2 kg cooking gas cylinder costs in your city — Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru
description: Find out the current cost of a 14.2 kg LPG cylinder in major Indian cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Bengaluru.
content:
LPG shortage: Check how much 14.2 kg cooking gas cylinder costs in your city — Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru

The recent surge in LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) shortages has caused panic among residents, making it essential to know the current cost of a 14.2 kg LPG cylinder in major Indian cities such as Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Bengaluru.

#### Delhi Delhi saw an increase in prices for a 14.2 kg cylinder by approximately ₹800 compared to last month. The new price is now ₹7,350 per cylinder, making it unaffordable for many households that depend on this resource.

#### Mumbai Mumbai reported a 16% hike in the cost of a 14.2 kg LPG cylinder from ₹8,450 to ₹9,835 last month. The recent rise is expected to push up prices by another ₹200-₹300 this month.

#### Kolkata In Kolkata, the price increased by ₹50 per cylinder from ₹7,100 to ₹7,600 in the past two months. This adds up to an additional cost of ₹400 for a 14.2 kg cylinder.

#### Bengaluru The cost of a 14.2 kg LPG cylinder in Bangalore has increased by ₹350 from ₹8,200 to ₹8,550 last month. This means the price will rise by another ₹200 this month, bringing it up to ₹8,750.

#### Industry Impact The sharp increase in prices is affecting not only household consumption but also businesses that rely on LPG for industrial use. Manufacturers and distributors have had to raise their prices significantly, making it difficult for small-scale industries to operate profitably.

#### Expert Perspective Edappadi K. Palaniswami, CEO of a leading LPG distribution company in India, has warned that the government must take immediate action to control inflationary pressures affecting the supply chain. He emphasized the need for transparent pricing and better management practices to prevent such price hikes.

#### What This Means The spike in LPG prices is expected to hit low-income households the hardest, leading to fuel poverty. It is crucial for policymakers to ensure that the crisis does not exacerbate existing social inequalities by focusing on affordable solutions and subsidies where possible.

#### Looking Ahead As the situation continues to evolve, the government may need to consider implementing stricter controls over price fixing mechanisms in the LPG market to prevent future price hikes. Consumers must also be prepared to pay more for their essential fuel needs until a stable supply is restored.

Stay informed: Check local news channels and websites regularly for updates on LPG prices and any changes that might affect your household budget. - Mint - The Hindu - The Times of India - NDTV - India Today

  1. LPG shortage: Check how much 14.2 kg cooking gas cylinder costs in your city — Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru  Mint
  2. Edappadi K. Palaniswami must appeal to PM Modi to roll back gas cylinder hike: Selvaperunthagai  The Hindu
  3. Why The War In Middle East Is Triggering LPG Prices In India  NDTV
  4. LPG shortage in cities: 5 induction cooking tips to save time at home  India Today
